title: RealHeights
description: Allow players to be their actual (real-life) height in Minecraft!
body:
![Dependencies](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/Dependencies.png?raw=true)
* [**BukkitOfUtils**](https://modrinth.com/plugin/bukkitofutils)

![Discord](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/JoinTheDiscord.png?raw=true)
Please join the Streamline Hub Discord in order to get updates and for me to fully assist you with bugs, questions, or suggestions.

Discord: [**click here**](https://drak.gg/discord)

![Summary](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/Summary.png?raw=true)
Allow players to be their actual (real-life) height in Minecraft!

![Why This?](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/WhyThis.png?raw=true)
Extremely easy to use with drag and drop functionality.

Ever wanted to see what your Minecraft friends would look like in real life when compared to you? Well, it isn't VR, but it sure is close! You get to see exactly how tall your friends are and there will definitely be a lot of good laughs when you find out who is actually the shortest, or tallest!

This plugin was officially made for Drak SMP: `play.drak.gg`

Comes with RESTful API hooks so if your players have used this plugin elsewhere, they can remain the same height from server to server. This is toggle-able of course! And, players will still have to **set their height upon first use**!

This also has database support for cross-network SMPs or height syncing.

![How to Install](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/HowToInstall.png?raw=true)
1. Download and install BukkitOfUtils. Found here: [**BukkitOfUtils**](https://modrinth.com/plugin/bukkitofutils)
2. Download the correct version of this plugin for you server version.
3. Place in your `plugins` folder.
4. Stop server.
5. Start server.
6. Use to your liking.

![More Info](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/MoreInfo.png?raw=true)
## How to set your height
1. Install plugin. (Shown above.)
2. `/heights set <integer or decimal>(i/c/f/m)` - where `(i/c/f/m)` is replaced with one of those characters (`i`= `inches`, `c` = `centimeters`, `f` = `feet`, and `m` = `meters`) and `<integer or decimal>` is replaced with a positive number (between `1 foot` and `9 feet` - or equivalent).

## Notes
* `i`= `inches`
* `c` = `centimeters`
* `f` = `feet`
* `m` = `meters`
* Defaults to feet if none of the above.

![Commands and Permissions](https://github.com/Streamline-Essentials/StreamlineWiki/blob/main/website/images/CommandsAndPermissions.png?raw=true)
## Argument Key
* `<required>` = argument described by "required" is required in the command
* `(optional)` = argument described by "optional" is optional in the command
* `(unit "i/c/f/m")` = argument described by "unit" is optional in the command and must be one of "i", "c", "f", or "m" **verbatim**.

## Commands
* `/heights`
  * `/heights set <integer or decimal>(unit "i/c/f/m") (player)`
    * Sets the height of a player or self.
  * `/heights get (player)`
    * Gets the height of a player or self.
  * `/heights reset (player)`
    * Resets the height of a player or self.
